cards.grammarNote: 에 marks static location with 있다. 집에 있어요 = I'm at home (just being there, no action).
에 marks the destination of movement verbs (가다, 오다, etc.). 학교에 가요 = I go to school.
에서 marks the location where an action takes place. 공부하다 is an active verb, so 에서 is required.
에서 marks the point of departure (where movement started from). 미국에서 왔어요 = came from America.
에 + 있어요 = to be at/in a place. No action is happening; just being there.
에서 + action verb (일하다). The work happens at the café, so 에서 is used.
에 marks destination with verbs of movement. 병원에 가요 = going to (toward) the hospital.
에서 marks where the action (reading) takes place.
어디서 = where + 에서 (contracted). 에서 marks departure/origin point.
Running is an active verb, so 에서 marks the park as the location of action.

cards.front: The book is on the table.
cards.grammarNote: 에 + 있어요 = existence. 위에 = on top. 테이블 위에 있어요 = is on the table.
만나다 (to meet) is an active verb, so 에서 is used to mark the meeting location.
에 + 있어요. The hotel exists at a place (downtown) — static location.
먹다 (eat) is active, so 에서 marks the restaurant as the action location.
기다리다 (wait) is active, so 에서 is used. The action of waiting happens at the bus stop.

cards.front: I am staying at a hotel.
cards.grammarNote: 묵다 (to stay/lodge) uses 에, not 에서, because lodging is more of a state than an ongoing active action. Note the progressive 고 있어요.
